summary.goal: Make the app fully accessible to users with disabilities by integrating with all macOS accessibility infrastructure — VoiceOver, keyboard navigation, dynamic type, and system display preferences
summary.description:
The app must be fully accessible to users with disabilities. macOS has excellent built-in accessibility infrastructure — the app must integrate with all of it.

## VoiceOver Support (Critical)
- All rendered markdown content must be accessible via VoiceOver
- Headings announced with proper heading level ("heading level 1", "heading level 2")
- Links announced as links with destination URL
- Images announced with alt text (if provided in markdown)
- Code blocks announced as code with language identifier
- Tables navigable cell-by-cell with row/column headers
- Lists announced with item count and nesting level
- The Open With button must have proper accessibility label and role
- Window title read correctly (filename + app name)

## Accessibility Hierarchy
- Use NSAccessibility protocol properly on all custom views
- Set accessibilityRole, accessibilityLabel, accessibilityValue on all elements
- Semantic structure: headings, paragraphs, lists, links, images all properly typed
- Reading order matches visual order (top-to-bottom, left-to-right)
- No content trapped in inaccessible containers

## Keyboard Navigation
- Full keyboard access (System Settings > Accessibility > Keyboard > Full Keyboard Access)
- Tab moves focus between actionable elements (Open With button, links)
- Arrow keys scroll content
- Space/Enter activates focused element
- Focus ring visible on focused elements (standard macOS blue ring)
- No keyboard traps — user can always Tab away

## Dynamic Type / Text Scaling
- Respect system text size settings where applicable
- Cmd+Plus/Minus for in-app font size adjustment
- Minimum readable font size: 9pt (never go below)
- Maximum useful font size: 36pt (beyond this, layout breaks)

## Reduce Motion
- Respect NSWorkspace.shared.accessibilityDisplayShouldReduceMotion
- When enabled: disable hover fade animations, use instant transitions for theme changes
- Scrolling physics unchanged (system handles this)

## Reduce Transparency
- Respect NSWorkspace.shared.accessibilityDisplayShouldReduceTransparency
- When enabled: make Open With button fully opaque instead of semi-transparent
- Ensure no content relies on backdrop blur for readability

## Increase Contrast
- Respect NSWorkspace.shared.accessibilityDisplayShouldIncreaseContrast
- When enabled: boost text/background contrast, thicken borders, reduce subtle color differences
- Apply to all themes (override theme colors if needed for compliance)

## Color Blindness
- Links distinguishable by underline, not just color
- Syntax highlighting tested against deuteranopia, protanopia, tritanopia
- Error/warning states (if any) use shape+text, not just color

## Testing Requirements
- All features tested with VoiceOver enabled
- Test with Full Keyboard Access enabled
- Test with Increase Contrast, Reduce Motion, Reduce Transparency each enabled
- Verify WCAG AA compliance for all themes (automated + manual)
- Test with macOS Accessibility Audit in Xcode

acceptance_criteria.description: All rendered markdown content navigable via VoiceOver with proper semantic roles
Full keyboard navigation works with Full Keyboard Access enabled
Respects Reduce Motion, Reduce Transparency, and Increase Contrast system settings
All themes pass WCAG AA contrast ratio requirements (4.5:1 body, 3:1 large text)

observations: NSAccessibility protocol on all custom views
Semantic structure: headings, paragraphs, lists, links, images properly typed
VoiceOver must announce heading levels, link destinations, alt text, code language
Focus ring visible on focused elements (standard macOS blue ring)
Reduce Motion: disable hover fade animations, instant transitions
Reduce Transparency: Open With button fully opaque
Increase Contrast: override theme colors if needed
Test each: Increase Contrast, Reduce Motion, Reduce Transparency
WCAG AA compliance for all themes (automated + manual)
Syntax highlighting tested against deuteranopia, protanopia, tritanopia

steps.title: Add semantic accessibility attributes to rendered markdown content
steps.description: Extend the MarkdownStyler to add accessibility attributes to the NSAttributedString output. Headings: add .accessibilityHeadingLevel attribute. Links: NSTextView handles link accessibility automatically via .link attribute. Images: add accessibilityLabel from alt text in markdown. Code blocks: add custom accessibility description with language identifier. Lists: annotate with list structure. This ensures VoiceOver can announce content semantically, not just as flat text.

acceptance_criteria.description: Headings have correct accessibilityHeadingLevel (1-6)
Images have accessibilityLabel from markdown alt text
Code blocks announced with language identifier
VoiceOver reads content in correct order (top to bottom)

architecture.code_block: Accessibility description: 'Code block, language: Swift' etc.
architecture.reading_order: NSTextView's natural reading order (sequential attributed string)

steps.title: Add accessibility labels to Open With button and custom views
steps.description: Set proper accessibility properties on all custom UI elements. The Open With button: accessibilityRole = .button, accessibilityLabel = 'Open With', accessibilityHelp = 'Opens this file in another application'. The reload banner (from system-integration): accessibilityRole = .group with label 'File changed notification' and the Reload button properly labeled. Settings window elements: theme cards with accessibilityLabel = theme name, font dropdown with proper label. Ensure Tab navigation includes the Open With button.

acceptance_criteria.description: Open With button has accessibilityRole=.button and descriptive label
Reload banner elements are accessible
Settings theme cards have theme name as accessibility label
Tab navigation includes all actionable elements

architecture.protocol: NSAccessibility protocol on custom NSView subclasses
architecture.tab_order: Open With button is in the key view loop (acceptsFirstResponder = true)

steps.title: Implement Increase Contrast theme overrides
steps.description: Observe NSWorkspace.shared.accessibilityDisplayShouldIncreaseContrast and when enabled, apply contrast-boosting overrides to the current theme. Increase text/background contrast ratios beyond WCAG AA to WCAG AAA (7:1). Thicken borders (blockquote left border, table borders, code block borders). Reduce subtle color differences — make heading colors more distinct from body text. Apply to all themes uniformly. Re-render all open windows when the setting changes.

acceptance_criteria.description: Increase Contrast mode boosts all color pairings to WCAG AAA (7:1)
Borders thickened when Increase Contrast enabled
Heading colors more distinct from body text
Changes applied dynamically when user toggles the system setting

architecture.overrides: ThemeManager applies contrast multiplier to theme colors
architecture.border_boost: Border widths increased by ~50% when Increase Contrast enabled
architecture.re_render: Notification triggers pipeline re-render for all windows

steps.title: Implement Reduce Transparency for Open With button
steps.description: Observe NSWorkspace.shared.accessibilityDisplayShouldReduceTransparency. When enabled, make the Open With button fully opaque instead of semi-transparent. Remove or reduce the backdrop blur effect (NSVisualEffectView). Ensure no content in the app relies on transparency or blur for readability. This is primarily about the floating Open With button overlay.

acceptance_criteria.description: Open With button fully opaque when Reduce Transparency enabled
Backdrop blur removed or reduced
All content readable without transparency effects
Changes applied dynamically when user toggles the setting

architecture.button_change: Set NSVisualEffectView.state = .inactive or use solid background color
architecture.dynamic: Observe and apply without requiring restart

steps.title: Verify and fix keyboard navigation for Full Keyboard Access
steps.description: With Full Keyboard Access enabled in System Settings, verify that all interactive elements are reachable via Tab. Tab order should be: content area (NSTextView) -> Open With button -> back to content. Within content, links should be focusable and activatable with Enter. Arrow keys scroll the content. Space activates focused elements. No keyboard traps — user can always Tab away from any element. Focus ring (standard macOS blue ring) visible on all focused elements.

acceptance_criteria.description: Tab cycles through all interactive elements in correct order
Links focusable and activatable with Enter
No keyboard traps — Tab always moves to next element
Focus ring visible on all focused elements
Arrow keys scroll content when content area is focused

architecture.key_view_loop: NSTextView.nextKeyView = Open With button, button.nextKeyView = NSTextView
architecture.link_focus: NSTextView link navigation via Tab within content
architecture.focus_ring: Standard drawFocusRingMask() on custom views, or focusRingType = .exterior
